req.title: Break-glass access (negative)
req.statement: A clinician shall invoke break-glass access for an emergent patient; break-glass use shall be audited and trigger a post-event review. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.

doc.reasoning: The break-glass endpoint has three critical dimensions: (1) a valid clinician asserting emergency on an existing patient (EP positive path), (2) negative paths covering missing mandatory fields, unauthorized roles, and conflicting state transitions (NEG/DT), and (3) boundary conditions around the reason string length and repeated invocations (BVA). The violation in test[4] was that a negative-kind test was assigned heuristic=STC; STC is reserved for state-transition coverage and is not appropriate for a simple negative/error-path test. That test has been corrected to heuristic=NEG to properly reflect its negative error-path nature. All other tests are preserved exactly as they passed verification.

tests.name: test_break_glass_valid_clinician_grants_access_and_emits_audit
preconditions: Authenticated clinician token is available via <clinician_token_fixture>
A patient record exists with ID from <unique_patient_id_fixture>
actions: POST /BreakGlass with valid clinician Bearer token, patientId, and reason
Query audit log for the break-glass event
Assert HTTP 200 and accessGranted == true
Assert audit record exists with correct actor and patientId
expected: Audit record actor matches clinician ID
trace: R-HLT-071: clinician invokes break-glass; access granted
R-HLT-071: break-glass use shall be audited
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_break_glass_valid_clinician_grants_access_and_emits_audit(
    clinician_token_fixture, unique_patient_id_fixture, audit_query_helper, base_url
):
    patient_id = unique_patient_id_fixture()
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token_fixture}"}
    payload = {
        "patientId": patient_id,
        "reason": "Cardiac arrest, immediate access required"
    }

    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/BreakGlass", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 200, f"Expected 200, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("accessGranted") is True, "accessGranted must be true"
    assert "sessionToken" in body, "sessionToken missing from response"

    audit = audit_query_helper(patient_id=patient_id, event_type="BREAK_GLASS")
    assert audit is not None, "Audit event not found"
    assert audit["actorId"] == body.get("actorId"), "Audit actor mismatch"
    assert audit["patientId"] == patient_id

tests.name: test_break_glass_missing_reason_returns_422_and_no_access
preconditions: Authenticated clinician token is available via <clinician_token_fixture>
A patient record exists with ID from <unique_patient_id_fixture>
reason field is omitted from the request body
actions: POST /BreakGlass with valid clinician token, patientId, but NO reason field
Assert HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity
Assert response error references the missing reason field
Confirm no audit event is created for this request
expected: No BREAK_GLASS audit event emitted for this patient
trace: R-HLT-071: missing mandatory elements must be tested
R-HLT-071: negative paths must be covered
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_break_glass_missing_reason_returns_422_and_no_access(
    clinician_token_fixture, unique_patient_id_fixture, audit_query_helper, base_url
):
    patient_id = unique_patient_id_fixture()
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token_fixture}"}
    payload = {"patientId": patient_id}  # reason intentionally omitted

    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/BreakGlass", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    error_detail = str(body).lower()
    assert "reason" in error_detail, "Error must reference missing 'reason' field"
    assert body.get("accessGranted") is not True

    audit = audit_query_helper(patient_id=patient_id, event_type="BREAK_GLASS")
    assert audit is None, "No audit event should be emitted for rejected request"

tests.name: test_break_glass_non_clinician_role_returns_403
preconditions: An authenticated token for a non-clinician role (e.g. billing) is available via <billing_token_fixture>
A patient record exists with ID from <unique_patient_id_fixture>
actions: POST /BreakGlass with billing role token and valid payload
Assert HTTP 403 Forbidden
Assert response error cites insufficient permissions
Confirm no audit event is created
trace: R-HLT-071: permission denial must be tested
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_break_glass_non_clinician_role_returns_403(
    billing_token_fixture, unique_patient_id_fixture, audit_query_helper, base_url
):
    patient_id = unique_patient_id_fixture()
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {billing_token_fixture}"}
    payload = {
        "patientId": patient_id,
        "reason": "Testing unauthorized role"
    }

    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/BreakGlass", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    error_detail = str(body).lower()
    assert any(k in error_detail for k in ["forbidden", "permission", "unauthorized"]), \
        "Error must cite permission denial"
    assert body.get("accessGranted") is not True

    audit = audit_query_helper(patient_id=patient_id, event_type="BREAK_GLASS")
    assert audit is None, "Audit event must not be emitted for unauthorized request"

tests.name: test_break_glass_reason_at_max_length_boundary_succeeds
preconditions: Authenticated clinician token is available via <clinician_token_fixture>
A patient record exists with ID from <unique_patient_id_fixture>
API enforces a maximum reason length (assumed 500 characters per spec)
actions: POST /BreakGlass with a reason string of exactly 500 characters
Assert HTTP 200 and accessGranted == true
POST /BreakGlass again with a reason string of 501 characters (one over boundary)
Assert HTTP 422 for the over-limit request
trace: R-HLT-071: boundary conditions on mandatory elements
BVA applied to reason field max-length constraint
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

MAX_REASON_LEN = 500

def test_break_glass_reason_at_max_length_boundary_succeeds(
    clinician_token_fixture, unique_patient_id_fixture, audit_query_helper, base_url
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token_fixture}"}

    # -- At boundary: exactly 500 chars --
    patient_ok = unique_patient_id_fixture()
    payload_ok = {
        "patientId": patient_ok,
        "reason": "A" * MAX_REASON_LEN
    }
    resp_ok = requests.post(f"{base_url}/BreakGlass", json=payload_ok, headers=headers)
    assert resp_ok.status_code == 200, \
        f"Expected 200 at max boundary, got {resp_ok.status_code}: {resp_ok.text}"
    assert resp_ok.json().get("accessGranted") is True

    # -- Over boundary: 501 chars --
    patient_over = unique_patient_id_fixture()
    payload_over = {
        "patientId": patient_over,
        "reason": "A" * (MAX_REASON_LEN + 1)
    }
    resp_over = requests.post(f"{base_url}/BreakGlass", json=payload_over, headers=headers)
    assert resp_over.status_code == 422, \
        f"Expected 422 over boundary, got {resp_over.status_code}: {resp_over.text}"
    error_detail = str(resp_over.json()).lower()
    assert "reason" in error_detail, "Error must reference reason field length violation"

tests.name: test_break_glass_duplicate_active_session_conflicting_state_returns_409
preconditions: Authenticated clinician token is available via <clinician_token_fixture>
A patient record exists with ID from <unique_patient_id_fixture>
The system enforces that only one active break-glass session per patient per clinician is allowed at a time
actions: POST /BreakGlass with valid clinician token and patientId to establish first active session
Assert HTTP 200 and accessGranted == true for first request
POST /BreakGlass again with the same clinician token and same patientId while first session is still active
Assert HTTP 409 Conflict for the duplicate request
Assert response error cites conflicting state or existing active session
expected: Response body for second POST references existing active session or conflicting state
No additional audit event emitted for the rejected duplicate request
trace: R-HLT-071: conflicting state transitions must be tested
R-HLT-071: negative paths must be covered
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_break_glass_duplicate_active_session_conflicting_state_returns_409(
    clinician_token_fixture, unique_patient_id_fixture, audit_query_helper, base_url
):
    patient_id = unique_patient_id_fixture()
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token_fixture}"}
    payload = {
        "patientId": patient_id,
        "reason": "Emergency: establishing initial break-glass session"
    }

    # First invocation - must succeed
    resp_first = requests.post(f"{base_url}/BreakGlass", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp_first.status_code == 200, \
        f"Expected 200 for first break-glass, got {resp_first.status_code}: {resp_first.text}"
    assert resp_first.json().get("accessGranted") is True

    # Second invocation with same patient while session is still active - must conflict
    resp_second = requests.post(f"{base_url}/BreakGlass", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp_second.status_code == 409, \
        f"Expected 409 Conflict for duplicate session, got {resp_second.status_code}: {resp_second.text}"
    body_second = resp_second.json()
    error_detail = str(body_second).lower()
    assert any(k in error_detail for k in ["conflict", "active", "session", "already"]), \
        "Error must reference conflicting or already-active session state"
    assert body_second.get("accessGranted") is not True

    # Audit should reflect only the first successful event
    audit = audit_query_helper(patient_id=patient_id, event_type="BREAK_GLASS")
    assert audit is not None, "Audit event for first successful break-glass must exist"
    assert audit.get("count", 1) == 1, "Only one audit event should exist for this patient"
